_doc:
Phase 11 demo — auto-layout for imported circuits.

Generated by importing the hand-written 2:1 MUX from examples/hdl-corpus/phase8-mux2-handwritten.v through the full pipeline:

  text  →  parseVerilog (Phase 8)  →  AST
        →  elaborate (Phase 9)      →  IR
        →  toCircuit (Phase 10)    →  raw scene at (0,0)
        →  autoLayout (Phase 11)   →  this scene with x/y placed

What Phase 11 ships:
  • Sugiyama-style topological layering — INPUTs/CLOCK on the leftmost column, OUTPUTs on the rightmost, every other node gets a column equal to (max driver column + 1).
  • Clock wires are skipped during depth computation, so a sequential feedback loop (always @(posedge clk) q <= d → q drives d back) does not create infinite columns.
  • Within each column, nodes spread evenly along the y axis with stable id-sort order — two imports of the same .v file produce the same coordinates.
  • Cycle-tolerant — the iterative depth pass is bounded to 2N iterations and falls through gracefully on back-edges.

Open this scene to see the imported circuit on a real grid: clk + sel + d0 + d1 on the left, the inferred MUX in the middle, the FF/REGISTER on its right, q output on the rightmost edge. Run node examples/tests/test-hdl-layout.mjs for the unit suite (14 checks: deterministic placement, column ordering, no-overlap, opt-out flag).
